reviews.text:
Our original set of these sheets were purchased in September 2015, we got the navy blue color. That has been our main set of sheets that we have used ever since. When we wash them, they go right back on the bed. Now granted, we aren't the type to have several different sets in rotation, but we have never felt the need to replace them with anything else.

I recently bought the light blue shade for a different bed and I'm sure we will love those too. If I had any suggestions though, I wish that this set would come with four pillowcases or have an option to pay more to choose a set that comes with 4. Also, it wasn't an issue before with the navy, but this light blue color is just a bit on the sheer side. You can see the pattern of the pillow through it. Not a huge deal, its for a childs bed. But it would be nice if it was just a bit more opaque.

reviews.title: bath rugs
reviews.text: I purchased 4, 2 smaller, 2 large.  First the color - I chose the taupe gray.  It is exactly as described - browner than gray - but not at all like the photo.  The photo looks much more gray.  I actually needed a taupe so I rolled the dice.  Turned out right for me.  Second, the material of the mat. The nap is very - how shall I describe it - furry.  It is not a loop carpet.  The fibers are a bit frayed at the ends so it ends up feeling and looking a tad furry. Plus the strands are a bit longer - adding to the fur like look.  I am still on the fence of the look of that - but I could see how those things may make the fibers more absorbent. We shall see.  I am hoping they don't crush too easily and recover after each use.  It is rubber backed (they note on the tag that you want to be sure not to have water underneath the rug as it could "slide on the water" Hmmm - I'll watch out for that - but it IS a bathroom rug - with water.)  They did wash up in the washing machine (I used the delicate cycle) and low temp in the tumble portion of my dryer closet.  No issues and very little lent - again, a bit of furry like fuzz.  Having said all of this, I never hesitate to update my reviews should new info seem useful.

reviews.title: Worked well for our outdoor event
reviews.text:
I ordered a total of 14 tablecloths in white, 12 round and 2 rectangle, for an outdoor event. The quality was suitable for our purposes. The fabric is sort of shiny and a bit slippery, but it drapes well. There are some quality control issues with the size not being entirely consistent on all of them, but it was not enough to be noticeable when the tablecloths were on the tables. They arrived quite creased, but since I didn’t want to iron 14 tablecloths, I threw them in the dryer, a few at a time, with a couple of damp washcloths, turned the dryer on low, and ran it for about 15 minutes. The deep creases disappeared and the tablecloths looked quite good. After our event, we had some stains - chocolate cake, colored frosting, etc. - which I pretreated. After washing, the stains all disappeared so I would agree that the tablecloths are pretty stain resistant.
Overall, I feel that these are good for the price, and are good for large events.

reviews.title: Excellent Water Bottle All Around
reviews.text:
Wow. This water bottle kicks butt. Let me tell you why.

(1) First and foremost, it keeps drinks cool (or warm) for HOURS, even when it's in a warm (or cool) environment. I put 6 ice cubes in there and filled it up to the top with water. And my water stayed icy cold for, like, 24 hours. Can you believe it?! Neither can I.
(2) It comes with 3 different lids. So, depending how I'm feeling a particular week or depending what I'm doing (hiking vs camping vs sitting at home, etc), I can easily change out the lid to whatever I feel like.
(3) The bottle itself has a decently wide lip. So, you can clean it fairly well with your hand (if you have small hands & wrists) or with a long scrub brush.
(4) It comes with a little straw scrub brush. To me, this is super important because cleaning little straws would otherwise be impossible and a pain in the butt.
(5) At least one of the lids has a handle that I can hook a belayers hook onto. Very nice for, you know, when I'm doing adventurous stuff like kayaking and hiking and what not.
(6) It is durable. Again, when I'm doing adventurous stuff (or even when I'm sitting at home, let's be honest) it is always good to have a durable water bottle.
(7) The colors look awesome. I got the light blue fades to darker blue color.
(8) It carries a decent amount of liquid. Though it doesn't fit in a cup holder, I need a bigger water bottle like this to force myself to drink more water (cuz it's good for you!).

All in all, I love this water bottle and give it 5/5 stars.
